en.title: Early Childhood
en.desc: Explore child development from birth through age eight. Students learn to create nurturing, developmentally appropriate learning environments and graduate with credentials aligned to careers in childcare, preschool, and early intervention.

postsec.name: Child Development Associate (CDA)
postsec.note: The most widely recognized credential in early childhood education; can be earned during or after completing this sequence.

postsec.name: AAS in Early Childhood Education
postsec.note: 2-year degree that qualifies graduates to serve as lead teachers in licensed childcare centers and preschools.

postsec.name: BS in Child Development / Early Childhood Education
postsec.note: 4-year degree for students pursuing preschool director, school counselor, or child life specialist roles.

postsec.name: Early Intervention Specialist
postsec.note: State-issued credential for working with infants and toddlers (ages 0–3) who have developmental delays or disabilities.

careers.title: Preschool Teacher
careers.desc: Plan and implement developmentally appropriate activities for children ages 3–5 in preschool or Head Start programs.

careers.title: Childcare Center Director
careers.desc: Manage the operations, staff, curriculum, and licensing compliance of a childcare facility.

careers.title: Kindergarten Teacher
careers.desc: Teach foundational literacy, numeracy, and social skills to students in K-level classrooms.

careers.title: Child Life Specialist
careers.desc: Support children and families in hospital or clinical settings using therapeutic play-based approaches.

financial.name: T.E.A.C.H. Early Childhood Scholarship
financial.note: Covers tuition, books, and travel for childcare workers pursuing ECE credentials — often includes a paid completion bonus.

financial.name: CDA Scholarship Program
financial.note: The Council for Professional Recognition offers financial assistance specifically toward the CDA credential for qualifying candidates.

financial.name: Head Start Training Grants
financial.note: Head Start grantees receive dedicated funds for staff professional development and continuing education.

financial.name: Federal Pell Grant
financial.note: Up to $7,395/year for eligible students. Apply via FAFSA at studentaid.gov.

es.title: Primera Infancia
es.desc: Explora el desarrollo infantil desde el nacimiento hasta los ocho años. Los estudiantes aprenden a crear entornos de aprendizaje nutritivos y apropiados para el desarrollo.

postsec.name: Asociado de Desarrollo Infantil (CDA)
postsec.note: La credencial más reconocida en educación de primera infancia.

postsec.name: AAS en Educación de Primera Infancia
postsec.note: Título de 2 años que califica a los graduados para ser maestros principales en centros de cuidado infantil.

postsec.name: BS en Desarrollo Infantil
postsec.note: Título de 4 años para estudiantes que buscan ser directores de preescolar o especialistas en vida infantil.

postsec.name: Especialista en Intervención Temprana
postsec.note: Credencial estatal para trabajar con bebés y niños pequeños (0–3 años) con retrasos del desarrollo.

careers.title: Maestra de Preescolar
careers.desc: Planifica e implementa actividades apropiadas para el desarrollo de niños de 3 a 5 años.

careers.title: Director/a de Centro de Cuidado Infantil
careers.desc: Gestiona las operaciones, el personal, el currículo y el cumplimiento de licencias de una guardería.

careers.title: Maestra de Jardín de Infantes
careers.desc: Enseña alfabetización, numeración y habilidades sociales fundamentales a estudiantes de nivel K.

careers.title: Especialista en Vida Infantil
careers.desc: Apoya a niños y familias en entornos hospitalarios usando enfoques terapéuticos basados en el juego.

financial.name: Beca T.E.A.C.H. de Primera Infancia
financial.note: Cubre matrícula, libros y transporte para trabajadores de cuidado infantil que buscan credenciales ECE.

financial.name: Programa de Becas CDA
financial.note: El Consejo para el Reconocimiento Profesional ofrece asistencia financiera para la credencial CDA.

financial.name: Becas de Capacitación Head Start
financial.note: Los beneficiarios de Head Start reciben fondos para el desarrollo profesional del personal.

financial.name: Beca Federal Pell
financial.note: Hasta $7,395/año para estudiantes elegibles. Solicitar vía FAFSA en studentaid.gov.
